orgPolicy.title: Hire error notifications moving to pod Slack channels
orgPolicy.summary: HRIS is shifting recruiter-related hire error triage from individual DMs to dedicated pod Slack channels (e.g., R&D, CX). Recruiters will be added to their pod channel and tagged when action is needed.
bullets: Watch for being added to your pod channel; HRIS will @-tag the recruiter who needs to act.
Goal is more scalable, transparent hire-error triage.

tp.point: Hire-error pings are moving to pod channels — keep an eye on the pod channel, not your DMs.

orgPolicy.title: In-office designation method simplifying to a single 65-mile radius
orgPolicy.summary: Leadership is merging the two existing methods (commute time + MSA boundaries) into one standard: a 65-mile radius (130-mile diameter) from each hub — the current comp-band method. Commute-in approvals are paused where the candidate's comp zone differs from the office.
bullets: Only 1 employee fell outside 65 miles but under 120 min, so both methods tell the same story.
Recruiting has STOPPED approving candidates to commute in when their comp zone differs from the office (also impacts benefits).
Ask from Tracie/Kristin: confirm whether you use anything other than the standard zip-code sheet.

tp.point: Don't approve commute-in candidates whose comp zone differs from the office — flag to your PE.

orgPolicy.title: FY27 Company KRs — ratification expected July 1
orgPolicy.summary: Five objectives, including a new Product/Agentic category focused on Gus (Gusto's agentic platform). Objective 5 is most relevant to Invite: job family consolidation and hiring within Gusto hubs.
bullets: DRIs are finalizing target numbers ahead of the State of G.
Four KRs directly affect how and where we hire.
More detail shared at the People Team All Hands.

tp.point: FY27 goals land ~July 1; expect hiring focus on job-family consolidation and Gusto hubs.

orgPolicy.title: Cycle 2 People Team priorities set
orgPolicy.summary: Two themes anchor C2: (1) AI-Native Gusties — role redesign and raising the talent bar across the lifecycle; (2) Modernize Delivery of People Work — vendor decisions (scheduling tool, Risotto replacement, VMS), getting off Lattice, policy/practices repo, and total-rewards automation.
bullets: "Raising the Talent Bar" spans pre-hire (VMA+ evolution), PE enablement, and FY27 talent-system design.
Kristin Ruehle and Dashini Jeyathurai are named DRIs.

orgPolicy.title: InviteOS Release 3 is live
orgPolicy.summary: First wave of Cycle 2 launches: a Debrief Agent that auto-organizes interview scorecard data into a structured debrief summary, and a Feature Release Tracker for submitting/voting/tracking builds. PeopleOS Release Notes now documented in Notion.
bullets: Debrief Agent cuts manual debrief prep — review/edit the AI summary before debriefs.
Ongoing ask: log into PeopleOS via Okta daily, explore, and submit feedback.

tp.point: Try the new Debrief Agent in InviteOS — it auto-builds your debrief summary from scorecards.

orgPolicy.title: Greenhouse elevated self-service for Invite PEs
orgPolicy.summary: Invite PEs now have elevated Greenhouse permissions: create Evergreen & Confidential reqs directly, and close/re-open reqs — no ticket required. EIB bulk req uploads are now processed by the HRIS Ops team (still submit via PHC).
bullets: No more tickets to open/close/reopen reqs or to spin up Evergreen/Confidential reqs.
Bulk req-upload questions now go to HRIS Ops.

orgPolicy.title: Laura Lockwood on leave — Elan Bailey is day-to-day POC
orgPolicy.summary: Effective June 22, Elan Bailey is the day-to-day point of contact for People Central, with Saumya Chopra and Kristin Ruehle available for escalations.
bullets: Anything that normally goes to Laura should loop in Elan first.

orgPolicy.title: Offer-letter SOP updates: broker license + California relocation
orgPolicy.summary: Two SOP changes from the prior IOPE entry remain relevant: updated broker-license requirement verbiage for CX & Sales offers, and a new 'relocating to California?' dropdown that drives CA-specific relocation (clawback) language.
bullets: Select Yes/No on the new California relocation field so the correct documents generate.
Broker-license verbiage now auto-populates when 'Does this role require a broker license?' = Yes.

orgPolicy.title: Workday ↔ Google Calendar integration live
orgPolicy.summary: Time off submitted in Workday now auto-syncs to your calendar and your PE's calendar — no more manual calendar blocks.

orgPolicy.title: IOPE current focus areas
orgPolicy.summary: Invite Ops in-flight workstreams: recruiter intake process redesign, a Hiring Committee Checklist Agent, the Invite Targets Tracker, and a near-complete IKB revamp (migration to Notion).
bullets: No Action Required items and no new SOPs in the 6/29 newsletter.

items.heading: Event assignments out; book your event
bullets: Jaime pre-assigned everyone to an onsite event based on survey rankings, with an Invite PE POC per pod.
Book your event (start after 5:30 PM), arrange dinner if not included, budget $250/person.
Monday 7/13 run of show: office AM, final onsite prep, lunch/hang, Pedal Hopper around RiNo, welcome party at Bierstadt Lagerhaus.
Onsite comms moved to #temp-invite-onsite-july-2026.

items.heading: Project Orbit launched (quarterly PE mentorship)
bullets: Quarterly mentorship/coaching program pairing FTEs with a PE mentor; matching survey was due Fri 6/26.
One required coaching 1:1 per quarter; pairings rotate.

items.heading: Invite PE Hub launched in Notion
bullets: Central place for cascade-to-team items, PE action items, parking lot, and weekly meeting notes — replaces hunting across Slack/Docs.
React with :clipboard: in #invite-pes to log an action item automatically.

items.heading: People Team All Hands — Fri 6/26
bullets: Covered FY27 Company Goals + how People fits in, Cycle 2 initiatives, and PeopleOS demos.

items.heading: CX Benefits Advising hiring sprint closed
bullets: 30 hires with a 7/6 start date — through a new role type, pre-announcement hiring, TBD comp band, and a mid-sprint pivot to licensed-only candidates.

items.heading: Gusto closed Friday, July 3
bullets: Plan candidate pipelines, offers, and deadlines around the closure.
